title: 2026 MLB Opening Day Schedule, Scores: Live Updates with Dodgers-Diamondbacks, Mariners-Guardians Action
body:
As the crack of the bat echoes across the nation, Major League Baseball's highly anticipated 2026 regular season is finally underway, marking the beginning of a thrilling 162-game marathon. Opening Day brought a plethora of exciting matchups, showcasing the best of the league's talent in a spectacular display of skill and strategy. With 11 games on the docket, fans were treated to a packed schedule filled with captivating pitching battles and intriguing storylines.

One of the most anticipated matchups of the day saw the Los Angeles Dodgers take on the Arizona Diamondbacks, with the Dodgers emerging victorious in a closely contested game. The Dodgers' strong starting rotation, bolstered by a dominant performance from ace Clayton Kershaw, proved to be too much for the Diamondbacks to handle. Meanwhile, other marquee matchups across the league saw the Seattle Mariners face off against the Cleveland Guardians, with the Mariners coming out on top in a thrilling 5-4 victory.

As the season gets underway, fans are eager to witness the emergence of new stars and the resurgence of veteran players. The Yankees' shutout victory over the San Francisco Giants was particularly notable, solidifying the team's position as one of the top contenders in the American League East. The Giants, meanwhile, will look to bounce back from a disappointing opening performance and recapture their momentum from last season.

The opening day also saw several key storylines unfold, including the return of injured stars and the debut of highly touted rookies. The Chicago Cubs, one of the most improved teams in the offseason, kicked off their campaign with a convincing 7-3 win over the St. Louis Cardinals. The Cubs' young core, bolstered by the addition of talented rookies like outfielder Ian Happ, looks poised to make a deep run in the postseason.

In addition to the on-field action, Opening Day also marked a milestone for the game itself, with several historic moments and milestones achieved. The Kansas City Royals, for example, celebrated the 50th anniversary of their 1976 World Series championship, while the Boston Red Sox retired the number 42 jersey of legendary pitcher David Ortiz in a moving ceremony.

As the 2026 regular season begins, fans are in for a treat, with a packed schedule of thrilling matchups and storylines set to unfold over the next six months. With the playoffs just on the horizon, every game will count, and the competition is sure to be fierce. As the season gets underway, one thing is clear: this is shaping up to be a year to remember for baseball enthusiasts around the world. With the drama, excitement, and unpredictability that defines America's favorite pastime, the 2026 season promises to be one for the ages.
